Literal.Value:
'import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Professional color palette
COLORS = {
    "gray_900": "#212529",
    "gray_600": "#6C757D",
    "gray_500": "#868E96",
    "positive": "#1971c2",
    "negative": "#c2255c"
}

# Calculate metrics
current = dataset["Order Lines"].sum()
prior_year = dataset["Order Lines (PY)"].sum()
percent_change = dataset["Order Lines vs. PY %"].mean()

# Format value to K format
def format_thousands(val):
    if val >= 100000:
        return f"{round(val/1000)}K"
    elif val >= 10000:
        return f"{val/1000:.1f}K"
    else:
        return f"{val/1000:.2f}K"

# Determine performance color and arrow
if percent_change > 0:
    perf_color = COLORS["positive"]
    arrow = "\u25B2"
else:
    perf_color = COLORS["negative"]
    arrow = "\u25BC"

# Create figure
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(3.6, 2.3))
ax.set_xlim(0, 1)
ax.set_ylim(0, 1)
ax.axis("off")

# Label at top
ax.text(
    0.5, 0.85,
    "Order Lines",
    ha="center", va="center",
    fontsize=10,
    fontweight="bold",
    color=COLORS["gray_600"]
)

# Main value
ax.text(
    0.5, 0.55,
    format_thousands(current),
    ha="center", va="center",
    fontsize=28,
    fontweight="bold",
    color=COLORS["gray_900"]
)

# Percentage change with arrow
ax.text(
    0.5, 0.30,
    f"{arrow} {abs(percent_change)*100:+.1f}%",
    ha="center", va="center",
    fontsize=14,
    fontweight="bold",
    color=perf_color
)

# Comparison text
ax.text(
    0.5, 0.12,
    f"vs. {format_thousands(prior_year)} (PY)",
    ha="center", va="center",
    fontsize=8,
    color=COLORS["gray_500"]
)

plt.tight_layout(pad=0)
plt.show()'
